tweets.text: .@SecBlinken is now on @Spotify! Check out his latest recommended playlist, listen to some tunes from around the world, and follow the Secretary here: https://t.co/vgL6HJeucr https://t.co/GEDbQDVSGg

The United States is contributing $580 million to 7 multilateral partners to support the critical work they are doing to help end the COVID-19 pandemic, strengthen public health systems, and provide urgent relief. https://t.co/U3bzTlXDJj https://t.co/69RRm1a5P1
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver more than 2.5 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to our friends in Angola and Ghana. https://t.co/cmddD4ZUsf

Planning to travel abroad in the new year? Be sure you're up to date on travel requirements before you go, and ​check the U.S. Embassy's website for country specific COVID-19 information. https://t.co/HcTCr8iLBe

Have you heard the news? @SecBlinken is now on @Spotify! Check out his latest recommended playlist, listen to some tunes from around the world, and follow the Secretary here: https://t.co/z5SyMl0ki7. https://t.co/xlDsd66O0o

Vaccines are vital to ending the COVID-19 pandemic. This week, the United States delivered nearly 5 million additional doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Pakistan via #COVAX, with our total donations to Pakistan now reaching over 37 million doses. https://t.co/zYoOrLQrHy

.@SecBlinken said in Jakarta that the United States and Indonesia must work together to stop the COVID-19 pandemic. This week, the U.S. partnered with #COVAX to deliver 3 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Indonesia, in addition to the 25 million doses already provided.

Explore the science and gendered impacts of heat waves in the latest DipNote blog from @StateGWI and learn more about how the U.S. is mobilizing a whole-of-society approach to encourage collaboration in tackling the climate crisis: https://t.co/UyTFsdukHO

America is leading the world on addressing the climate crisis and we will continue on this path. If we succeed, we will capitalize on the greatest opportunity to create quality jobs in generations, build a more equitable, healthy, and sustainable society, and protect our planet. https://t.co/c4LyQ9JC05

.@SciDiplomacyUSA Asst. Secretary Medina reflects on 2021, reaffirming OES' core mission: to provide American leadership to conserve and protect the global environment, ocean, health, and space for the prosperity, peace, and security of future generations. https://t.co/ZbJgAcJ1UL
.@SecBlinken: The United States is focusing on how we can more effectively fight corruption, which President Biden designated as a core U.S. national security interest. https://t.co/vbFKCm4ket

.@SecBlinken discusses how the U.S. is making investments in improving the lives and welfare of people across the Western Hemisphere to tackle the economic and social challenges facing our people. https://t.co/tTEFsXxd0g

.@SecBlinken announced Rina Amiri as the first Special Envoy for Afghan Women, Girls, and At-Risk Populations. She will join our efforts in supporting a stable and secure Afghanistan, working to ensure inclusion of women and marginalized communities. https://t.co/r4SHZ5Asno

.@SecBlinken: Americans and Africans share a yearning to live in places where their rights are respected, their voices are heard, and their governments answer to them and deliver for them. Working together, we can support democracy – in our countries and around the world. https://t.co/CUc3YLZxAS

The U.S. is helping Africa to build a more stable and inclusive economy through initiatives such as Prosper Africa, our Africa Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA), and the Build Back Better World initiative. https://t.co/GTHhlKuPES

This year, the United States has worked to strengthen our partnerships across Africa in ways that serve Africa’s interests, our interests, and the interests of people worldwide whose lives and futures depend in part on what we can achieve together. https://t.co/xsNgVC312G

The United States has invested hundreds of millions of dollars in clean energy and climate resilience projects throughout the Indo-Pacific region, providing clean, green jobs in both the United States and throughout the Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/W6O6aexy7Z

The United States has long been, is, and always will be an Indo-Pacific nation. We are committed to the people and governments of the region who want more, better opportunities for all of their people and will continue to work with our partners to make that a reality. https://t.co/eEL2eAyneK

The United States will advance a free and open Indo-Pacific in which problems will be dealt with openly; rules will be reached transparently and applied fairly; and goods, ideas, and people will flow freely across land, cyberspace, and the open seas. https://t.co/0RTqfKXtNx

In today’s briefing, @StateDeptSpox condemned the Burmese regime’s continuing escalation of violence, echoed @SecBlinken’s call for Russia to release Paul Whelan and Trevor Reed unconditionally, and addressed the closure of International Memorial. More: https://t.co/3bdKqqJuTy

What does revitalizing our alliances and partnerships look like? It’s the United States showing up to the table, engaging with our partners on key international issues, standing shoulder–to-shoulder with them, and leading with diplomacy. https://t.co/0VlewchccA

.@SecBlinken reflects on this past year: "None of the really big challenges that we face and that affect the lives of Americans – from COVID to climate to the disruptive impact of new technologies – can be solved by any one country working alone." Remarks: https://t.co/MTx8e6pqAV https://t.co/3xjyuMP4dG

The United States reaffirms our commitment to helping end the COVID-19 pandemic, in partnership with #COVAX. We delivered 2.5 million additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Bangladesh, bringing our total donations to Bangladesh to over 21 million doses. https://t.co/kveJPY08L1

In 2021, @SecBlinken outlined how the Department will deliver on a foreign policy for the American people. Our diplomats and global workforce answered that call to serve. In 2022, we will continue making progress on that promise and leading with diplomacy. https://t.co/Ya2MpA66hX
Follow along with us as we join @SecBlinken in taking a look back at some highlights from 2021. Keep an eye on this space for a recap of major speeches to important foreign policy initiatives.

.@SecBlinken at #SummitForDemocracy Young Democratic Leaders event: “The strength of our democracies depends on their success, and it depends on getting more young people to join them–voting, running for office, getting involved in civic life, in making our democracies better.” https://t.co/xKJfoOS6uh

From February 17-18, 2022, @EngageState will host the 13th Annual Virtual HBCU Foreign Policy Conference. Learn more and sign up for conference updates here: https://t.co/d1Ay0v7D11. #SavetheDate https://t.co/k6qndTkZFZ
.@SecBlinken on addressing the challenge to end the COVID-19 pandemic: “Stopping COVID requires transparency, sharing data, being forthright with citizens.” https://t.co/Z957gLwIrn
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver more than 5 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 9 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/0rlWLsXcab

December is vaccination month in Ghana, and this week, the United States delivered 1.7 million additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the Ghanaian people. Partnering with @gavi #COVAX and @_AfricanUnion, we have now provided Ghana with over 7 million vaccine doses. https://t.co/e9xSnUac8h

.@SecBlinken: We’re on stronger footing when it comes to COVID, climate, and other urgent challenges. And we’re in a stronger geopolitical position to deal with countries like China and Russia, as they seek to undermine the international system. https://t.co/HR42uCkak5
Today, we send wishes to everyone celebrating Kwanzaa! May your celebrations be filled with light during this special time. Happy Kwanzaa! https://t.co/wc0biY6bRN

The United States delivered 1.5 million additional Pfizer vaccine doses to the people of Egypt, bringing the total doses delivered over the past 4 months to nearly 15 million. We are working with @gavi #COVAX, and other global partners, to protect people worldwide from COVID-19.
Merry Christmas! 🎄 🎁 We wish all who are celebrating a joyous Christmas day! May this time bring you peace, blessings, and time with family and friends. https://t.co/xEvDjX4m9w

The United States delivered 664,560 additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Nepal via #COVAX, bringing total U.S. donations to Nepal to over 2.2 million doses. We are committed to protecting the people of Nepal from COVID-19 and to combatting the pandemic globally. https://t.co/SyLHpCJjU6

.@POTUS: The United States is committed to strengthening our democracy at home and to working with parties around the world, to prove that democracies can deliver for people on issues that matter most to them. https://t.co/zAJu2ryH1W

.@SecBlinken at #SummitForDemocracy Young Democratic Leaders event: “The strength of our democracies depends on their success, and it depends on getting more young people to join them–voting, running for office, getting involved in civic life, in making our democracies better.” https://t.co/2fP6v3h1lh

From February 17-18, 2022, @EngageState will host the 13th Annual Virtual HBCU Foreign Policy Conference. Learn more and sign up for conference updates here: https://t.co/d1Ay0v7D11. #SavetheDate https://t.co/o4uA5xNxE1

.@SecBlinken: We’re building our capacity to lead in areas of diplomacy that will matter more and more to our people in the years ahead, like global health, like climate, like technology, like economics. https://t.co/njKk5nbEkh
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver more than 5 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 9 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/4YEmlmFBty

.@SecBlinken on ending America’s longest war: “This is the first time in 20 years that no U.S. troops are spending the holidays in Afghanistan, and we’re not sending a third generation of American soldiers to fight and die there.” Full remarks: https://t.co/MTx8e67PJn https://t.co/bZaATsndrd

The United States is contributing $580 million to 7 multilateral partners to support the critical work they are doing to help end the COVID-19 pandemic, strengthen public health systems, and provide urgent relief. https://t.co/gL8f9cEQGP

.@SecBlinken: We’re on stronger footing when it comes to COVID, climate, and other urgent challenges. And we’re in a stronger geopolitical position to deal with countries like China and Russia, as they seek to undermine the international system. https://t.co/wsWtlaTJfL

As @POTUS said, the United States is tougher than COVID-19 because we have the power of science and vaccines that prevent illness and save lives, and tougher because of our resolve. We will continue to lead the global effort to end the COVID-19 pandemic. https://t.co/byTaSBJQP2

The U.S. has provided 350 million COVID-19 vaccine doses and nearly $20 billion in assistance to combat COVID-19 globally. Our assistance is rooted in science, based on need, in collaboration with international and regional institutions, and with no political strings attached. https://t.co/MNoJzN5I1Q

.@SecBlinken: As we see happening right now, with the rise of the Omicron variant – none of us will be safe until all of us are safe. We still have a long way to go to beat the COVID-19 pandemic. But let’s not lose sight of the fact that we’ve come very far. https://t.co/xeWPN2cCtT

.@SecBlinken: We said we’d restore American leadership in the climate crisis. Well, on day one, we rejoined the Paris Agreement. We raised global ambitions to reduce emissions through major investments in climate finance – including quadrupling our own funding. https://t.co/OplUEjDWVC

.@StateEUR’s Assistant Secretary Donfried on diplomacy with Russia: “We will not compromise the key principles on which European security is built, including that all countries have the right to decide their own foreign and security policy course free from outside interference.” https://t.co/IPlvGDFR1R

The U.S. delivered 5 million additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Pakistan via #COVAX, bringing our total donations to Pakistan to over 32.6 million doses. Our joint efforts to provide safe, effective, and free vaccines will help stop the spread of COVID-19 and save lives. https://t.co/teH9h4v13s

Vaccines are critical to stopping the spread of COVID-19. Today the United States delivered an additional 821,340 Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Sri Lanka via #COVAX, bringing our total donations to the people of Sri Lanka to over 3.4 million doses. https://t.co/f9Oe7c2bC4

.@SecBlinken: We’ll keep working toward the goal @POTUS set in September at the global COVID summit that he convened: Vaccinating 70% of the world by next fall. And we’ll keep leading the push for greater global health security. https://t.co/bEngCGCzRU

.@SecBlinken: We’re much more aligned with our allies and partners now than we were a year ago on nearly every issue, including Russia’s aggression toward Ukraine and its neighbors, Iran’s nuclear program, and China’s efforts to challenge the rules-based international order. https://t.co/bkByk6DCTO
The United States highly values our Transatlantic relationships, and we are committed to remaining in lockstep with our European allies. https://t.co/YL79FNGRMS https://t.co/QGg7l3CYPg

Nuclear science and technology play a key role in our efforts to protect the world's natural resources. Learn how researchers are using nuclear science to monitor, measure, and better protect the planet upon which we all depend.

.@SecBlinken reflects on this past year: "None of the really big challenges that we face and that affect the lives of Americans – from COVID to climate to the disruptive impact of new technologies – can be solved by any one country working alone." Remarks: https://t.co/MTx8e67PJn https://t.co/IwOrRNCCK0

The United States delivered 613,340 COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Lebanon. We reaffirm our commitment to saving lives worldwide and helping end the pandemic through our partnership with #COVAX. https://t.co/bbEIFwQVFF

The U.S. delivered over 1.77 million additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Bangladesh via #COVAX, bringing our total donations to Bangladesh to more than 18.5 million doses. These safe, effective, and free vaccines are saving lives and helping stop the spread of COVID-19. https://t.co/0qrGBdRcd4

The United States, in partnership with #COVAX, provided an additional 3.4 million doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the Philippines to help end the pandemic. This delivery is part of the U.S. commitment to share 1.2 billion vaccine doses with the world. https://t.co/4sZr0KkDWx

.@StateEUR Assistant Secretary Donfried reiterated the message we have heard from our @NATO Allies: Any further Russian aggression against Ukraine would have massive consequences and carry a high price. https://t.co/tuL0WzfIoR https://t.co/1GJSzkKJaZ

.@SecBlinken discussed how democracies need to stand together against dangerous health security trends, because if we don’t, no one will. Read more here: https://t.co/9nFNlVcmTS. https://t.co/7tSjL6Chrq

Multilateral institutions are essential partners in the fight against COVID-19. Read more on the United States' $580 million contribution to @WHO, @UNICEF, @pahowho, @UNDP, @UNFPA, @FAO, and @UN_Women: https://t.co/61fykPNcsP.

The United States is contributing $580 million to 7 multilateral partners to support the critical work they are doing to help end the COVID-19 pandemic, strengthen public health systems, and provide urgent relief. https://t.co/SYFgEw9WqY
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks to the press from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/apZzQaP2Ao

Today at approximately 1:30 p.m. EST, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks to the press from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/WhYM61arID. https://t.co/ludkWRfa8A
.@SecBlinken on addressing the challenge to end the COVID-19 pandemic: “Stopping COVID requires transparency, sharing data, being forthright with citizens”. https://t.co/XRaWg4UDaL

Today, @SecBlinken delivered remarks at the Toys for Tots presentation with @StateDeptDSS, thanking Department employees for their donations and the Marines for their work to protect Department employees and​ make Christmas brighter for children. https://t.co/Gf4GFkwt3O

Today the United States delivered 1,178,190 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Libya via @gavi #COVAX. We are committed to the equitable distribution of vaccines worldwide and protecting the people of Libya through safe and effective vaccines. https://t.co/Zbn9Geitoq

The United States delivered an additional 100,620 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Maldives in partnership with #COVAX. Our joint efforts to protect families and communities from COVID-19 are vital to helping end this pandemic in Maldives and beyond. https://t.co/lKfkpyQHjd

The United States and #COVAX delivered more than 318,000 additional Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to our neighbors in the Caribbean. Each vaccination brings us one step closer to ending the pandemic and being able to share time together in the new year in safety and health. https://t.co/8hytAP0lFB
Please join the U.S. Department of State in welcoming our new Afghan neighbors to the United States. Learn how you can help at https://t.co/756UJoQRaS.

With today's shipment of 168,000 COVID-19 vaccine doses, the United States has now delivered 319,200 Johnson & Johnson doses to Yemen through #COVAX. These vaccines are in addition to the nearly $4 billion we have provided in humanitarian assistance to the people of Yemen. https://t.co/rrE2kPc2CF

.@StateDeptSpox expresses disappointment at the verdicts issued in Egypt: Journalists, human rights defenders, and others seeking to peacefully exercise their freedom of expression should be able to do so without facing criminal penalties, intimidation, harassment, or reprisal. https://t.co/4Tribwdwt2

.@StateDeptSpox: We congratulate the people of Chile on the exemplary Dec 19 democratic presidential runoff election, & President-elect @GabrielBoric for his decisive victory. We look forward to working with the incoming Boric administration to continue our important partnership. https://t.co/5t0pRDQDpJ

.@StateDeptSpox announces that @SecBlinken has designated @UnderSecStateJ as the Special Coordinator for Tibetan Issues to coordinate U.S. government policies, programs, and projects supporting Tibetans and protecting their rich culture and traditions. https://t.co/tCNDiDCkRw https://t.co/8ZButHNJOz
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State, also streaming live on https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz and https://t.co/xKV5NCEO0H. https://t.co/TEgi1UN5MV
Looking to learn more about how you can help welcome our Afghan allies in your community this holiday season? The @WhiteHouse explains how here: https://t.co/lmryeVVYnV. #OperationAlliesWelcome

No matter where you are in the United States, you and your neighbors can help welcome arriving Afghan families through the new @sponsorship_hub Sponsor Circle program for Afghans. Learn how by visiting https://t.co/EkVgbAhCUJ. https://t.co/AZjzw6MtCw

Happy 2nd birthday to @SpaceForceDOD! Today we celebrate the women and men who serve to protect U.S. and allied interests in the rapidly evolving domain of space. #HBDSpaceForce https://t.co/taKKHQAaym
This past week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver over 4.9 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 11 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/aMPLvXPtDc

.@POTUS: Let us together reaffirm our determination that the future will belong to those who embrace human dignity, not those who trample it; who unleash the potential of their people, not those who stifle it; and who give their people the ability to breathe free. https://t.co/3LhWkt6AWQ

The United States will advance a free and open Indo-Pacific in which problems will be dealt with openly; rules will be reached transparently and applied fairly; and goods, ideas, and people will flow freely across land, cyberspace, and the open seas. https://t.co/5qIT42JynQ

What happens in the Indo-Pacific will shape the trajectory of the world in the 21st century. It accounts for 60 percent of the world economy, two-thirds of all economic growth over the last five years. Learn more: https://t.co/KyjLcpq1DU. https://t.co/Xk3kQMLOhe

.@VP: “I believe our world is at the start of a new era – an era with new challenges, an era with new opportunities, an era that is defined by interconnection and interdependence.” Full remarks: https://t.co/HuAleLMeM9 https://t.co/W0vaKyjGyE

From February 17-18, 2022, @EngageatState will host the 13th Annual Virtual HBCU Foreign Policy Conference. Learn more and sign up for conference updates here: https://t.co/d1Ay0v7D11. #SavetheDate https://t.co/E7t6UVD8Kr

.@POTUS: Liberty knows no borders. It speaks every language. It lives in anti-corruption activists, human rights defenders, journalists, peaceful protestors on the frontlines of this struggle all around the world. More: https://t.co/RX9MntcO3g https://t.co/JtkiAWjYUa

.@VP: Together, we must protect the rights of people with disabilities, the rights of people of color, the rights of Indigenous peoples, LGBTQI+ rights, and women’s rights…let us do the work that democracy requires. Let us go to work, let us deliver together for the people. https://t.co/17p7MTo8TS

Have you heard the news? @SecBlinken is now on @Spotify! Check out his latest recommended playlist, listen to some tunes from around the world, and follow the Secretary here: https://t.co/z5SyMkIJqz. https://t.co/5YbwKwrjCk

.@POTUS on democracy here in the U.S. and around the world: “The choices we make ...in this moment are going to fundamentally determine the direction our world is going to take in the coming decades.” https://t.co/GR7i5FMttD

On #InternationalMigrantsDay and every day, the United States continues to address the root causes of irregular migration, and advocate for safe, legal, and humane migration. Read the full statement from @SecBlinken: https://t.co/VVa4QHEeWu https://t.co/btQwaHQk5N

.@POTUS: As we continue to work at home to bring the United States closer to what we call a “more perfect union,” we’re doubling down on our engagement with and support of democracies around the world. https://t.co/hZJZDHQzfT

.@POTUS: Democracy is hard. We all know that…But it’s the best way to unleash human potential and defend human dignity and solve big problems.  And it’s up to us to prove that. https://t.co/sTo5vlIo00

The United States donated an additional 299,520 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Tajikistan via #COVAX. Our COVID-19 vaccine assistance to the people of Tajikistan is vital to stopping the spread of the virus and to saving lives. https://t.co/EoubdPZru0

During today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed @SecBlinken’s recent trip to the Indo-Pacific, welcomed the release of the individuals held hostage in Haiti, and highlighted @StateEUR’s Asst Sec Donfried’s visits to Kyiv, Moscow, and Brussels. More: https://t.co/6YHMOEdZ4W.
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver over 4.9 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 11 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/RgsHPydGoV

To create strong and resilient democracies, all people of all genders must have opportunities to reach their full potential. In the latest DipNote from @StateGWI, read about efforts outlined at the #SummitForDemocracy to advance gender equity & equality.  https://t.co/yWqLd2Zv4P

.@SecBlinken: We will help build a more resilient Indo-Pacific. The COVID-19 pandemic and the climate crisis have underscored the urgency of that task. It has also inflicted a massive economic toll, from shuttered factories to the halt of tourism https://t.co/ia1k67JRQ4
.@POTUS: “Democracy is what makes it possible for hope and history to rhyme. And today, hope and history lie in our hands.” Full remarks: https://t.co/RX9MntuoUO. https://t.co/iG914GZgFZ

.@POTUS: The United States is committed to strengthening our democracy at home and to working with parties around the world, to prove that democracies can deliver for people on issues that matter most to them. https://t.co/gAUSuPa4qv

“After witnessing firsthand many COVID-19 illnesses and deaths in my neighborhood and the community at large, I resolved to protect myself and my family." Read more about why Zambian citizens are encouraging their peers to get vaccinated: https://t.co/f0nF8dehMe.

The United States delivered an additional 204,750 Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Jamaica. This third shipment brings our total donation to Jamaica to more than 615,000 doses and reaffirms our commitment to support Jamaica’s fight against COVID-19. https://t.co/uIwBzo4vbq

The United States continues its commitment to saving lives and helping end the pandemic through vaccine donations. In partnership with #COVAX, we delivered an additional 999,180 Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the Bolivian people. https://t.co/iw2XQKvbrM

The United States is committed to the equitable distribution of COVID-19 vaccines worldwide. We have delivered nearly 320 million vaccine doses to more than 110 countries​ with no strings attached. More on how we are leading the fight against COVID-19: https://t.co/pkhvSWcdmF.

The U.S. is announcing 10 new pledges at the @Refugees #HLOM to enhance protection and solutions for refugees worldwide and refugees/stateless persons in the U.S., in addition to the $200 million initial contribution to UNHCR’s 2022 Global Appeal. https://t.co/9z1s1sOXFF

The United States has sent more than 100 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to the Indo-Pacific and provided over $2.8 billion in additional assistance to the region to save lives. We have done so free of charge, with no strings attached. https://t.co/NRy0yFgxuD

The United States has invested hundreds of millions of dollars in clean energy and climate resilience projects throughout the Indo-Pacific region, providing clean, green jobs in both the United States and throughout the Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/r79x57f2EX

.@SecBlinken: People across the Indo-Pacific are already feeling its catastrophic impact: 70 percent of the world’s natural disasters strike in this region, and over 90 million people in the region were affected by climate-related disasters in 2019. https://t.co/8eZB7KUUVn

.@SecBlinken: The United States has already provided more than $1 trillion in foreign direct investment in the Indo-Pacific. The region has told us loud and clear that it wants us to do more. We intend to meet that call. https://t.co/CtZ6GRYuCZ

At the first Summit for Democracy, @USUN Ambassador Linda Thomas-Greenfield met with young people from more than 60 countries. Learn more about their discussion and why young voices are critical to making democracy work. https://t.co/uaQLlVNBU6

From February 17-18, 2022, @EngageState will host the 13th Annual Virtual HBCU Foreign Policy Conference. Learn more and sign up for conference updates here: https://t.co/d1Ay0v7D11. #SavetheDate https://t.co/xfZkgZbtb6

.@SecBlinken on a free and open Indo-Pacific: Threats are evolving. Our security approach has to evolve with them. We’ll seek closer civilian security cooperation to tackle challenges ranging from violent extremism, to illegal fishing, to human trafficking. https://t.co/N0HtJzoMUL

The United States supports Malaysia in its efforts to promote maritime security and governance. The U.S. sends more than 300 Malaysian service members to various academies and schools in the United States each year through International Military Education and Training programs. https://t.co/YwjQGSmhDI
Threats are evolving, and our security approach must evolve with them. To do that, we will lean on our greatest strength: our alliances and partnerships. https://t.co/2IZWXEhpUb

Malaysia continues to be a key trading partner and an attractive location for U.S. economic and commercial activity. Last year, our goods and services trade with Malaysia totaled an estimated $60.1 billion. https://t.co/qTpViDV2AF

The U.S. and Malaysia mutually benefit from our close people-to-people ties. The U.S. has hosted over 1,600 Malaysian @StateIVLP participants since 1952, and over 4,400 Malaysians are @ExchangeAlumni of ECA-sponsored exchange programs in Malaysia. https://t.co/Y321suW3Nu

Over the past 64 years of Malaysia’s independence, our countries have forged a strong partnership rooted in close people-to-people, economic, and security ties. Learn more about the U.S.-Malaysia bilateral relationship: https://t.co/4PTsgKfGhz https://t.co/Yz4S3xZSdl
The United States has already provided more than a trillion dollars in foreign direct investment in the Indo-Pacific, and we will meet the call from the region to do more. https://t.co/P4CqWYRaeA

.@SecBlinken: Freedom is about the ability to write your future and have a say in what happens in your community and your country, no matter who you are or who you know. And openness naturally flows from freedom. https://t.co/dOYyS8fT6B

The United States will deepen our treaty alliances with Australia, Japan, Republic of Korea, the Philippines, and Thailand. We will foster greater cooperation among these allies and seek ways to knit our allies together with our partners – as we have done with the Quad. https://t.co/MdDCukoTeB

.@SecBlinken: We’ll deepen our treaty alliances with Japan, the Republic of Korea, Australia, the Philippines, and Thailand. Those bonds have long provided the foundation for peace, security, and prosperity in the region. More: https://t.co/KyjLcpq1DU. https://t.co/LjAiXBaWQY

The United States will advance a free and open Indo-Pacific in which problems will be dealt with openly; rules will be reached transparently and applied fairly; and goods, ideas, and people will flow freely across land, cyberspace, and the open seas. https://t.co/6OJjgwu8Ym

.@SecBlinken: What happens in the Indo-Pacific will shape the trajectory of the world in the 21st century. It accounts for 60 percent of the world economy, two-thirds of all economic growth over the last five years. Learn more: https://t.co/KyjLcpq1DU. https://t.co/YO3HIIjDNG

#DYK that nuclear technology can be harnessed to improve human health? From preventing the spread of infectious disease to combating malnutrition, nuclear technology is used to address a variety of health issues. Read more in the latest DipNote from @StateISN.
